title: React Best Practices Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of modularity, functional components, accessibility, and file structure.

questions.question: Which and/or what is the Single Responsibility Principle for React components?
options: A component should only handle one thing and handle it well.
A component must have at least 500 lines of code.
A component can only be used once in an app.
None of the above.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about modern React component preference?
options: Class components are the industry standard for new apps.
Functional components with hooks are preferred over class components.
Both are exactly the same in performance and readability.
None of the above.

questions.question: What is the recommended approach if a component becomes very large (e.g., hundreds of lines)?
options: Ignore it and keep adding more code.
Break it down into smaller, simpler, and more testable sub-components.
Rewrite only the styling parts.
Add more comments to explain it.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key part of Accessibility (A11y)?
options: Using semantic HTML.
Providing alt text for images.
Using aria-labels for interactive elements.
All of the above.

questions.question: Is a consistent project file structure (e.g. putting components and pages in specific folders) useful for large teams?
options: Yes, it makes the codebase more navigable and maintainable.
No, it slows down the development process.
Yes, but only for mobile apps.
Only if you return them with an ID.
